englishDefinition: The way of life, ideas, customs, and arts of a group of people.
A public building where people learn about art, music, or local heritage.
Something, like medicine, that makes a sick person healthy again.
A strong wish to know about something.
Wanting to learn or know about something.
Happening now; of the present time.
Pieces of cloth hanging in front of a window to block light or view.
A way of doing things that has been used by a group for a long time.
A person who buys things from a shop or business.
To do or use less of something, especially food or money.
To stop or remove something, like a phone call or a piece of an object.
Pretty and pleasing in a small or charming way.
Happening every day.
Harm or injury that is done to a person or thing.
The chance that something bad or harmful will happen.
Likely to cause harm or injury.
Having little or no light.
Facts or information collected for study or use.
A particular day, month, and year.
A female child of a parent.
The early time of the morning when the sun first appears.
No longer alive.
Not able to hear, or having difficulty hearing.
An agreement, especially in business, between two or more people.
To take action to solve a problem or handle a situation.
The end of life of a person or animal.
To discuss a topic with opposing views, often in a formal way.
Money that one person or business owes to another.
To make up your mind about what to do.
A choice that you make after thinking carefully.
The flat outdoor floor of a ship where people can walk.
To add things to make a place look more attractive.
Something added to a place to make it look nicer.
To become smaller in size, number, or amount.
Going far down from the top or surface.
A unit for measuring temperature, angles, or amount.
To make something happen at a later time than planned.
Having a very pleasant taste or smell.
To take goods or letters to a place where they are needed.
A system of government in which the people choose their leaders by voting.
Based on the idea that all people are equal and can vote.
A doctor who takes care of people's teeth.
To say that something is not true.
One of the parts of a company, school, or large organization.
A large shop divided into many sections selling many kinds of goods.
To need or trust someone for help or support.
The distance from the top of something to the bottom.
To tell someone what something is like in words.
A statement that says what someone or something is like.
A large hot, dry area of land with very few plants, usually covered in sand.
To plan how something will look or work before making it.
A person whose job is to plan how new things will look or work.
A strong feeling of wanting something.
Sweet food eaten at the end of a meal.
The quality of trying hard to do something, even if it is difficult.
Wanting to do something so much that you will not let anything stop you.
To grow bigger, better, or more advanced.
The process of growing or becoming more advanced.
A very hard, clear stone that is very valuable, often used in jewelry.

A book that gives the meanings of words in alphabetical order.
To stop living.
Small cubes with dots on each side, used in board games.
The kinds of food that a person regularly eats.
The way in which two things are not the same.
Not the same; not alike.
Hard to do or understand.
A problem or something that is hard to do.

Careful and steady effort over time.
Working hard and carefully on a task.

Going straight from one place to another, with no stops or delays.
The way in which someone or something is moving or facing.
The person who tells actors what to do in a film, play, or show.
To go out of sight or stop existing.
To find something for the first time, or to learn a new fact.
The act of finding something new or learning a new fact.
To talk about a topic with other people.
A talk between people sharing their ideas about a topic.
Illnesses that affect the body or mind, often spreading easily.
A flat container used to hold or serve food.
Not telling the truth or doing things that are not fair.
The amount of space between two places or things.
Far away in space or time.

Feeling that you are spinning or about to fall.
To help someone with something they need.
A person whose job is to treat sick or injured people.
An official paper or computer file with important information.
A toy made to look like a small person.

A friendly sea mammal with a long nose, known to be very smart.
An expression telling someone not to end the phone call yet.
A grey or brown animal like a small horse, with long ears.
A small round mark or spot.
